text: Hi and welcome to This is My Architecture. My name is Liz and today I am here with Bashira from IHS
Market. Hey Bashira, how's it going? Doing great. Well thanks for joining us today. Happy to be here.
Can you tell me a little bit about IHS Market? Yeah, IHS Market offers information, analytics,
technology, and expertise to our customers so that it can be operationally efficient and make
well-informed decisions. Excellent and today we are going to be talking about a virtual software
training, right? Yep, so basically we have a product called Kingdom. It's a petrotechnical application
and it's a very, it requires high-end configurations with GPU support to make it run and what we're
doing is we're using AppStream to deliver that software and basically whenever it's time for
class students just click on a streaming URL and that's it it's ready to go. So they can one click
log into AppStream from pretty much any lightweight end-user device? Yep, they actually they don't even
have to log in it's a streaming URL so it's just Kingdom on the browser right there. So what does
that architecture look like then? Okay, so for in order for Kingdom to run it has a license server
and we're using an EC2 for that and for Kingdom projects the storage for that we are using a file
system for fss for the file system and then rds for the database and basically all of that together
makes Kingdom run. So if an instructor is going to set up one of these classes what does the instructor
need to do? Okay, so these three resources is what gets created for every single class, right? And we
wanted to give the instructors a very easy way to have this running quickly without starting days in
advance and just you know accruing costs when it's not needed. So we've provided them with CloudFormation
templates so that they can customize the images the way they need to and also create the resources very
quickly. So you mentioned that there are a few different kinds of classes. What are in those
CloudFormation templates? Okay, so we are using well obviously these resources that we see RDS and FSx
and in addition to that you know the images needs to be customized right and we need to get that as
part of the CloudFormation template. So we have introduced a custom resource and basically what
this does is it triggers a Lambda function and we want to be able to remote into the AppStream's image
builder and make the customizations that we need, wait for the image to be created and that can take about
20 to 45 minutes but with Lambda we can do that for the 15 minute execution time max, right?
Yep, 15 minutes. So and we are using Systems Manager. So basically Lambda uses Systems Manager to run a command
on the EC2 right here and that does all the magic. It runs a script where most of the AppStream image builder.
One of the customizations that's really important is we have to modify a session script in the image
builder which will make sure that the FSx that just got provisioned in CloudFormation is mounted
whenever the customer logs onto AppStream so that they can have access to their project. Once that's done
the EC2 will send a signal back to CloudFormation and we have a wait condition and handle over here.
So once it meets those requirements then AppStream is provisioned using the image we just created.
So it sounds like that's a great way to ensure a really high quality user experience.
Are there any design considerations that have been made for this architecture?
Yeah, so like I mentioned when we're recommending configurations to customers when they're running
Kingdom on their desktops we ask for very sophisticated GPU supported CPUs, right? But for classes we don't need
to use that for every single workflow. It's going to be really expensive. So unless a customer is doing 3D
visualizations on billions of data points we are sizing our fleet accordingly. So the CloudFormation
template gives the instructors parameters to customize those as well. That's a great way to align with
cost optimization. Yep. Any other cost optimization moves you guys have made? Yeah, so that's like a very
easy switch we made with RDS. Usually we have a full SQL server that's running there but we realize that our
projects are very small for the training purposes so just switching from a full SQL server to a SQL Express
engine reduced our cost by like 80%. Wow. Well thank you for walking me through the architecture Bashir.
It's been very interesting to learn how IHS Market is enabling virtual instructor-led training for the Kingdom
Petr technical application. Thank you for joining us on This is My Architecture. I look forward to catching you next time.
Especially today.
